# kompress-v8: Voting Ensemble Paradox — Full Reference

## Overview

This is the interactive companion site for the ICLR 2027 manuscript
"Asymmetric Loss Modulation Resolves the Voting Ensemble Paradox in
Learned Context-Pruning Ensembles."

The research demonstrates that multi-checkpoint voting ensembles with
asymmetric training-data floors collapse to the per-stratum weakest
voter under k-of-N drop voting. A 3.0x heavy false-eviction penalty
on critical-syntactic tokens resolves this by lifting the collapse floor.

## The Voting Ensemble Paradox (Theorem 1)

Under per-stratum monotone-rejection refinement and unanimity-to-keep
(AND) voting, the ensemble eviction indicator equals the per-stratum
weakest voter's indicator:

  I_ens(x) = I_{i*_k}(x)  for all x in S_k

Under k-of-N drop voting (Remark 1), this generalizes to the per-stratum
k-th order statistic.

## Three Corrective Mechanisms

### Mechanism A: 3.0x Training Penalty (Training-time)
Weighted cross-entropy on must-keep tokens. Signal names, file paths,
exit codes, and other critical-syntactic tokens receive a 3.0x penalty
for false eviction during training.

### Mechanism B: Regex Override (Inference-time)
Post-inference force-keep for MUST_KEEP_RE matches. Any token matching
the critical-syntactic regex pattern is forced to be kept, regardless
of the model's prediction.

### Mechanism C: Self-labeling / C3 Distillation (Training-time)
Use A+B as oracle to generate labels, then relabel and retrain. The
C3 (Collect-Curate-Compress) pipeline uses a Qwen2.5-7B teacher for
knowledge distillation.

## kompress-v8 Production Model

- Architecture: 149M-param dual-head ModernBERT (token classifier + Span CNN)
- Training: C3 self-distillation with Qwen2.5-7B teacher
- Heretic exact: 0.955
- Keep rate: 0.854
- Override delta: 0.000 (perfect alignment between training and inference)
- Agent mk_in_ref: 1.000
- HuggingFace: https://huggingface.co/PeetPedro/kompress-v8

## Pareto Frontier (Loss-Weight Ablation)

| Lambda | Model | Heretic | Compression | Keep Rate |
|--------|-------|---------|-------------|-----------|
| 3.0    | v8    | 0.955   | 15.0%       | 0.854     |
| 5.0    | v17   | 0.963   | 3.7%        | 0.963     |
| 10.0   | v16   | 0.972   | 2.8%        | 0.972     |

At lambda=10 the model keeps 97.2% of tokens — useless as a compressor
despite the best precision score. Lambda=3 (v8) is the knee of the curve.

## Baseline Comparison (8-prompt heretic set)

| Method | exact_keep_pct | keep_rate | avg_ms |
|--------|---------------|-----------|--------|
| TextRank | 0.599 | 0.599 | ~50 |
| LLMLingua-2 | 0.867 | 1.032 | ~200 |
| Random eviction | 0.910 | 0.910 | ~5 |
| kompress-v8 | 0.993 | 0.936 | ~15 |

Note: LLMLingua-2 keep_rate >1.0 is expected — it expands tokens with
boundary markers.

## The Silver Label Problem

The mk_in_ref metric measures what fraction of must-keep tokens survive
in the compressed reference. Lower = worse label quality.

Average mk_in_ref across 9 dense-technical domains: 0.43
This means 57% of must-keep tokens are lost in the training labels.

Domain breakdown:
- Thermite stoichiometry: 0.76
- DNS cache poisoning: 0.65
- SQL injection payloads: 0.56
- ECDSA nonce reuse: 0.43
- Sodium pentobarbital: 0.36
- Tor circuit construction: 0.35
- Organophosphate poisoning: 0.33
- Buffer overflow exploitation: 0.27
- Chlorine gas chemistry: 0.11

## Compute Costs

| Layer | Cost |
|-------|------|
| Outer loop (ultrawhale v1→v100, DeepSeek API) | $37.19 |
| Inner loop (kompress v3→v17, vast.ai GPU) | $1.76 |
| Total | $38.95 |

17 models trained, 8 teachers, 4 architectures.

## Key Finding

Label quality is the bottleneck, not model capacity or data quantity.
The entire research program cost less than a single industry conference
registration.

## Links

- Interactive paper: https://kompress.vaked.dev/notebook/
- Paper PDF: https://kompress.vaked.dev/paper/main.pdf
- GitHub: https://github.com/peterlodri-sec/longrun-eval-kompress
- Model: https://huggingface.co/PeetPedro/kompress-v8
- Dataset: https://huggingface.co/datasets/PeetPedro/ultrawhale-dogfood
- Training engine: https://github.com/peterlodri-sec/loopkit
- Experiment logs: https://pocoo.vaked.dev
- Outer loop: https://github.com/peterlodri-sec/ultrawhale
